title: Alex Knorre
text:
I am an Assistant Professor in the Department of Criminology at the University of South Florida. I received my Ph.D. in Criminology (2023) and A.M. in Statistics (2022) from the University of Pennsylvania, and then worked as a Postdoctoral Research Fellow at Boston College. Before joining Ph.D. program, I studied Russian criminal justice as a researcher with the Institute for the Rule of Law, European University in St Petersburg. My work focuses on victimization, gun violence, illegal drugs, and policy evaluation. I have recently published in Justice Quarterly, Journal of Criminal Justice, Aggression & Violent Behavior, and Criminology & Public Policy. I currently serve an a Managing Editor for Journal of Quantitative Criminology.
My CV is available here: PDF, 120Kb
My profiles: LinkedIn, Github, Google Scholar.

title: Alex Knorre
text:
Data
I prepared and maintain the following datasets:
Replication package for ‘’Measuring Changes in Bias-Motivated Attacks: Evidence from Anti-Asian Violence During the COVID-19 Pandemic’’ (2025) on OSF
Replication package for ‘’Comparing risks of firearm-related death and injury among young adult males in US cities to wartime service in Iraq and Afghanistan’’ (2022) on Github
Russian Crime Victimization Survey, Wave I (2018) on Dataverse, Wave II (2021) on Dataverse
Homicide rates in Russian cities (2020), data and guide in Russian on Github
Also, check out the TV Shows app.
